DECLARATION (SUPPLEMENTAL) OF LYNDA-LEE DYER IN SUPPORT OF DEFTS PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION; MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS FILED BY DEFENDANT BANC OF AMERICA INVESTMENT SERVICES, INC. BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. BANK OF AMERICA CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BANC OF AMERICA INVESTMENT SERVICES, INC., BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., AND, BANK OF AMERICA CORPORATION (1) NOTICE OF PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION; AND, 2) MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION GRANTED AS TO ALL DEFENDANTS. MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS GRANTED AS TO ALL DEFENDANTS. THE TERMS OF THE APPLICATION/AGREEMENT ARE CLEARLY AND PRECISELY WRITTEN, AND BY SIGNING, PLAINTIFF EXPRESSLY AGREED THAT HE RECEIVED, READ, UNDERSTOOD AND AGREED TO ARBITRATION, AND ACKNOWLEDGED RECEIVING A COPY OF THE AGREEMENT WHICH CONTAINED A CLEAR AND UNAMBIGUOUS ARBITRATION CLAUSE. MR. KERN PERSONALLY HANDED A COPY OF THE CUSTOMER AGREEMENT TO PLAINTIFF. THE TERMS OF THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE ARE BINDING. FINRA WOULD BE THE MOST APPROPRIATE CURRENT FORUM TO ARBITRATE DISPUTES BECAUSE NASD IS NO LONGER IN EXISTENCE AND FINRA IS ITS SUCCESSOR. NO PROCEDURAL OR SUBSTANTIVE UNCONSCIONABILITY DEMONSTRATED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: MARIA LERTORA, CSR #11935

Notice Of Petition To Compel Arbitration; And 2) Motion To Stay Proceedings; Compendium Of Non California Authroites
Off Calendar Oct-22-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS BANC OF AMERICA INVESTMENT SERVICES, INC., BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., AND, BANK OF AMERICA CORPORATION (1) Notice Of Petition To Compel Arbitration; and, 2) Motion To Stay Proceedings. Appearances: Terese Y. Cannata, Esq. of Cannata Ching & O'Toole LLP (415) 409-8900, appearing for plaintiff and opposing party; Kelly J. Moynihan, Esq. of Keesal, Young & Logan appearing for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to compel arbitration granted as to all defendants. Motion to stay proceedings granted as to all defendants. The terms of the application/agreement are clearly and precisely written, and by signing, plaintiff expressly agreed that he received, read, understood and agreed to arbitration, and acknowledged receiving a copy of the agreement which contained a clear and unambiguous arbitration clause. Mr. Kern personally handed a copy of the Customer agreement to plaintiff. The terms of the arbitration clause are binding. FINRA would be the most appropriate current forum to arbitrate disputes because NASD is no longer in existence and FINRA is its successor. No procedural or substantive unconscionability demonstrated. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Maria Lertora, CSR #11935
